note: Company-page posts report link clicks (LinkedIn Pages API). Personal-profile posts do NOT expose per-post link clicks anywhere, and LinkedIn passes only a site-level 'linkedin.com' referrer (post blog links also go off-domain), so per-post profile clicks can't be attributed. Instead, the table below shows total website visits from LinkedIn measured in GA4 (8 YTD) and which pages they landed on.

posts.text:
If you run AU480, AU680, or AU5800 systems, this covers:

- Which reagent families work across each platform
- Lot management considerations specific to AU analyzers
- Key SKU mapping for OEM-compatible alternatives
- Common compatibility questions we get from labs

Link to blog https://lnkd.in/g6Q98aBa

Most labs think they have two ways to buy reagents: straight from the manufacturer, or from an authorized distributor.

The part nobody mentions: those two quotes are the same quote wearing different hats.
Authorized distributors sign distribution agreements with price floors built in. Quote below the floor, lose the authorization. So the "competitive" number you get back isn't competing with anything.
There's a third channel. And it sits outside that price fence entirely.

New article on what that means in practice, what the compliance picture actually looks like, and how to run a quick test on your top 10 SKUs before your next contract renews.

Link to the blog: https://lnkd.in/ghn4hW9P

Before signing or renewing a reagent contract, five questions worth answering:
What does the CPI clause allow, and is it capped?
Which SKUs in the contract are actually sole-sourced, and which ones aren't?
What has the ancillary spend (calibrators, controls, cuvettes, wash) looked like over the past 12 months, and was any of it negotiated separately?
What is the termination cost, and how many months of savings does it take to recover it?
Has a volume-pricing alternative ever been requested in writing?
Most labs that go through this exercise discover the headline discount and the actual cost of ownership are telling different stories. In one case study on the blog, a Beckman Coulter AU680 and Access 2 lab found 18.7% total monthly savings without switching analyzers. The biggest number, 28%, was on calibrators and controls, items that had been bought from the same vendor, at the same contracted price, for two years.

The full case study, a pre-renewal checklist, and a free contract review offer are at the link - https://lnkd.in/gCqVNJ8h
